concepts.finding: Applies Skip-gram with Negative Sampling (SGNS) to item-based collaborative filtering. Treats item sets/baskets as sequences (discarding temporal order). Each pair of items in the same set is a positive example. Window size = set size. Outperforms SVD for item similarity, especially for unpopular items (68% vs 58.4% genre classification accuracy).
concepts.implication: Keyword clustering can use Item2Vec-style co-occurrence within documents/queries as positive signals. The subsampling of frequent words and negative sampling by unigram^0.75 distribution helps with tail queries.
concepts.open_question: Do keywords in the same search session or document define a 'basket'? What constitutes a meaningful co-occurrence set?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Item2Vec on keyword co-occurrence within search sessions produces better intent clusters than matrix factorization (SVD) of keyword-keyword co-occurrence.
